what_this_repo_is.summary: AI-assisted framework for scientific software development: incremental Fortran→C++ translation plus code inspection/generation/update and tool-using agent workflows.
capabilities: Incremental Fortran → C++ translation (with interface-layer support)
Bounded execution→review loop over a task file with durable on-disk state

environment_variables.name: CODESCRIBE_MODEL
environment_variables.description: Default model name used when -m/--model is omitted.

environment_variables.name: OPENAI_COMP_BASEURL
environment_variables.description: Base URL of an OpenAI-compatible API (must include /v1).

environment_variables.name: OPENAI_COMP_PROVIDER
environment_variables.description: Provider label used for routing/auth policy (e.g. ollama, alcf-inference).

environment_variables.name: OPENAI_COMP_APIKEY
environment_variables.description: API key used by the client. Some local endpoints accept any placeholder.

model_setup.example_command: code-scribe inspect README.rst -m oaic-llama3.1 -q "Summarize this repo and list key entry points."

modes.description: Repeated execution→review cycles. Cross-loop state is carried in-memory; each cycle starts a fresh agent whose context is injected by the harness. On-disk artifacts under .codescribe/loop/ are for inspection and crash-resume only.

options.--workdir DIR: Root directory the agent is bounded to (default: cwd)

modes.description: Single agent session with unbounded tools (use with care). Supports --verbose/-v to stream tool calls to stdout and --log/--log-path to write TOML diagnostics.

notes: Bounded mode is a practical constraint layer, not an OS sandbox.
Bounded bash uses an allowlist and rejects shell metacharacters; some commands will fail by design.
The loop task file can extend the bash allowlist via a [tools] bash = [...] TOML section.

docs.description: Loop mode internals and on-disk artifacts
